card.intuition: A set of five design principles intended to make software designs more understandable, flexible, and maintainable.
card.analogy: Building a custom PC: every part is separate (Single Responsibility), easy to swap for a better version (Open/Closed), uses standard plugs (Interface Segregation), and any 'GPU' works as long as it fits the slot (Liskov Substitution).
when_to_use: Designing a large, long-lived object-oriented codebase
Refactoring messy 'God Objects' that do too many things
Building a plugin system where new features shouldn't break old ones
gotchas: Don't over-engineer! Applying all 5 principles to a 10-line script is a waste of time. These are tools to manage COMPLEXITY, not rules for every line of code.
Dependency Inversion (D) is NOT the same as Dependency Injection (the technique), though they usually go together.

steps.annotation: System overview — step through to trace the flow.
SRP: each class has one responsibility — one reason to be modified.
OCP: add new behaviour via extension (new classes), not modification.
LSP: subclasses must honour the contract of their parent class.
ISP: clients should not depend on methods they don't use.
DIP: high-level modules depend on interfaces, not concrete classes.

exercise.prompt: Identify the 'S' in SOLID: A class should have one, and only one, reason to change.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "single"
assert check("Single")
print("PASS")
